questions.question: Explain the client-server model with a neat diagram.
keyPoints: Define client as requester of services
Define server as provider of services
Mention examples like web browser (client) and web server

questions.question: Differentiate between thick client and thin client with examples.
keyPoints: Thick client: local processing, examples MS Office, games
Thin client: server-dependent, examples web browsers
Comparison: resource requirements, offline capability, maintenance

questions.question: List and explain any five types of servers.
What are the advantages of client-server architecture?
Explain the HTTP request-response cycle in client-server model.
keyPoints: Server sends HTTP response with status code

questions.question: What are the disadvantages of client-server model?
keyPoints: Network dependency - no work without network
Single point of failure - server down affects all

questions.question: Explain the concept of socket and port with reference to client-server communication.
keyPoints: Server binds to port and listens

questions.question: With a neat diagram, explain the client-server architecture in detail. Discuss different types of clients and servers with examples.
keyPoints: Types of clients: thick, thin, hybrid
Types of servers: web, database, file, mail, proxy, application

questions.question: Explain the working of web client and web server with the HTTP protocol. Include details about request methods, status codes, and common ports used.
keyPoints: Request methods: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
Status codes: 200 OK, 404 Not Found, 500 Error

questions.question: Design a client-server application for an online library management system. Specify client requirements, server functionality, protocols, and database interactions.
keyPoints: Client: GUI for book search, issue, return

questions.question: Compare peer-to-peer model with client-server model. When would you choose one over the other?
keyPoints: Client-server: centralized server, easier management, single point of failure
Peer-to-peer: distributed, no central server, nodes act as both
P2P advantages: resilience, no server cost, file sharing (BitTorrent)
CS advantages: security, control, easier backup
Use CS for: enterprise apps, databases, web services
Use P2P for: file sharing, blockchain, decentralized apps
